notes: Spec asserts the single contiguous working span across a failed tool call and its recovery within one turn: ready → working on the prompt, state stays working across all retry cycles (no flicker to ready or waiting between attempts), final working → ready. The kiro-cli recipe drives a deterministic failing-then-corrected tool sequence ('false' then 'echo recovered') in ONE turn. The parser sees one Prompt (user_message → working), a mid-turn AssistantMessage(toolUse) (assistant, stays working) + a ToolResults whose status != success (IsError flag, NO state transition), the corrective AssistantMessage(toolUse) + ToolResults, then a text-only AssistantMessage (turn_done → ready). The intermediate_working phase pins the no-flicker invariant with duration_at_least_ms 2000 — long enough to span both tool cycles given typical kiro-cli latency. If the parser ever surfaced an errored ToolResults as a transition to ready or waiting, this phase would fire. No model/cost/token assertions beyond agent: the kiro-cli JSONL carries none of those fields (sidecar holds credits/model, daemon does not read it), so verify observes model unknown, cost/tokens not non-zero — orthogonal to this state-arc scenario.

text: User prompt drives ready → working (spec bullet 1)

State stays working across the failing 'false' and the corrective 'echo recovered' — the errored ToolResults (status != success) must NOT flicker the session to ready or waiting before the recovery cycle (spec bullet 2)
After the final successful attempt: working → ready at the terminal text-only AssistantMessage (spec bullet 3)
